description: RGB shows dense coastal city with healthy vegetation on hillsides and no smoke or discoloration. SWIR shows the expected pink/magenta urban signature and bright-green vegetation patches, with no fire glow or burn-scar darkening.
labeler_notes: Pre-event baseline image; useful as negative example.

description: RGB is dominated by open ocean with only a tiny sliver of coastline in the bottom-right corner. SWIR is almost entirely black (ocean), confirming negligible land coverage.
labeler_notes: Offset tile falls mostly over water; very little informational value.

description: RGB shows the urban hillside area at the event-peak date with muted brownish tones in upper slopes but no clear smoke plume. SWIR displays the usual pink urban signature with no bright-red fire glow and no unambiguous dark burn scar visible at this resolution.
labeler_notes: Acquired on the reported event-peak date but active fire signal is not confirmed visually; could be partially recovered or fire outside tile.

description: RGB shows open ocean with three ships near the bottom-right corner; SWIR confirms the vessels as small bright specks with no thermal anomaly or slick signature around them.
labeler_notes: Ocean offset tile; ships are routine vessel traffic.

description: RGB reveals noticeably darker, brownish-grey patches on the upper hillsides consistent with burn scarring not present in earlier dates. SWIR shows those same areas as comparatively low-reflectance zones amid the surrounding pink urban/vegetation mix, supporting a burn-scar interpretation.
labeler_notes: Post-fire burn scar visible; no active fire glow present, placing this as HIGH (aftermath) rather than CRITICAL.

description: RGB shows a single red cargo ship on open ocean; SWIR shows a single faint bright spot at the same location with no thermal or slick anomaly.
labeler_notes: Ocean tile with routine vessel; no hazard signal.

description: RGB shows moderate-to-heavy cloud cover over the city and hillsides, limiting ground visibility to small gaps. SWIR confirms a thick cloud deck (bright white) over most of the scene with only fragments of urban terrain visible in the lower portion.
labeler_notes: Clouds obscure the burn-scar area of interest; not usable for hazard assessment.

description: RGB is almost entirely covered by dense cumulus cloud over the ocean tile. SWIR confirms near-total cloud cover with no ground signal discernible.
labeler_notes: Ocean tile fully obscured by cloud; no usable content.

description: RGB shows a dry coastal city on pale limestone with a visible dry wadi channel and no floodwater or damage; SWIR shows a yellow/cream urban signature and sparse green patches with no anomalous moisture or damage signal.
labeler_notes: Pre-flood baseline; wadi is completely dry two weeks before the catastrophic flood event.

description: RGB is entirely uniform dark navy with no discernible land content; SWIR is entirely black, confirming this tile is pure open ocean or a no-data region.
labeler_notes: No land content in frame; tile is unusable.
